history.attempted: F4 was closed, the owner gate was recorded blocked, and autonomous retry was explicitly prohibited.
history.consequence: Each unchanged scheduler wake reloads context and risks an invalid repeated no-progress outcome.
history.requested_action: Pause the forge-heartbeat automation until the owner either approves F5 and selects a storage lane or intentionally resumes it.

history.attempted: Primary standards research, reuse comparison, adversarial failure analysis, fixed-vector manifest, recovery rules, and a recommended lane are complete.
history.consequence: Implementation without confirmation would silently lock permanent logical identity, save migration, serialization, hash, and deterministic stream semantics.
history.requested_action: Approve the recommended stable-logical-identity and deterministic CBOR/SHA-256/HKDF-HMAC lane, or request a different invariant.

history.attempted: Primary-source reuse research, fixed-point and floating-point comparison, Philox/PCG/Threefry/ChaCha/HMAC alternatives, cache mapping, adversarial fixtures, cost and recovery analysis are complete.
history.consequence: Implementation without confirmation would silently lock permanent numerical, cache, generator mapping, migration, and accelerator-equivalence semantics.
history.requested_action: Approve the bounded Q32.32/Q16.48 fixed-point reference and proposed Philox4x32-10 mapping lane, or request a different numerical or counter-generator lane.

history.attempted: Primary-source comparison, immutable descriptor and finite window design, delta ordering/conflict alternatives, snapshot/migration recovery, adversarial fixtures, and authority boundaries are complete.
history.consequence: Implementation without confirmation would silently lock persistent player/world lineage, conflict, migration, retention, and recovery semantics.
history.requested_action: Approve immutable descriptors, finite child windows, and the single-writer append-only parent-linked delta chain with non-destructive snapshots, or request different merge/retention semantics.

history.attempted: The fixed survival-pack master specification and bounded inheritance package were re-inspected alongside external primary sources; accepted product truth was separated from prototype-only engineering defaults.
history.consequence: The project architecture is already settled; implementation still needs bounded authority because it creates versioned lineage and recovery semantics.
history.requested_action: Approve building the conservative engine-neutral test harness for the already accepted lazy-universe and baseline-plus-delta architecture.

history.attempted: Fixed recovered packages and targeted legacy failures were inspected in memory, primary neutral standards and perception research were reconciled, the whole P4-P6 dependency chain was challenged, a premature registry status was repaired, and focused plus full gates passed.
history.consequence: Implementing more than P7a now could permanently lock quality to a first format, renderer, category table, synthetic score, or unsafe asset resolver. The staged boundary avoids that commitment.
history.requested_action: Approve only the tiny offline capability-free P7a contract and lineage test harness; keep all geometry, rendering, visual review, assets, animation generation, formats, runtime, and engine work gated.

history.attempted: The P7b-1b stable-LPAC design, critical review, exact owner question, and full Forge verification are complete. One unchanged owner-gate wake was recorded no_work. Automation update or deletion is unavailable in the current tool surface, so that limitation is recorded once and the batch is blocked rather than retried.
history.consequence: The denial canary cannot begin without exact owner authority, while further unchanged scheduler wakes would reload context and exceed the batch's one-no-progress limit.
history.requested_action: Approve or decline the single synthetic LPAC denial-canary trial. If deciding later, pause forge-heartbeat until the decision and resume it afterward.
